strategy.summary: Prioritize high-intent channels that do not require an existing audience: founder-led outbound, partner distribution, and operator communities. Support them with tactical content and service-assisted onboarding.
primary_wedge: Position against spreadsheets, agency-built reporting, and confusing BI tools
Sell outcomes like weekly clarity, margin visibility, and faster decisions
Bundle setup, reporting audits, or dashboard migration into the initial motion

why: One partner can unlock multiple buyers
Can be repurposed into outbound and community replies
Too slow to be the main engine initially
Usually weak at day 1 without reviews or pull

emphasize: see true channel and SKU performance
decision-ready reporting instead of dashboard clutter
reduce analyst and agency reporting labor

days_1_30: build a list of 100 merchants and 50 partners
create a sample report and audit checklist
days_31_60: double down on the best pain angle
tighten landing page and demo narrative
days_61_90: formalize partner program if pull exists
turn best content asset into lead capture

recommended_initial_mix: community participation with tactical content reuse
final_recommendation: Start with direct merchant outreach and partner-led distribution, using communities for trust and content as support. Focus on a narrow merchant segment with acute reporting pain and sell a concrete operational outcome rather than generic analytics value.
